title: Honoring the Unknown Heroes
story: Soldiers rushed into battle, risking everything to protect their country. Sadly, some never returned home and couldn't be identified. To honor these brave heroes, a special ceremony took place in 1958. On May 30, unknown soldiers who died in World War II and the Korean War were laid to rest at Arlington National Cemetery. This sacred place reminds us of the sacrifices made by those who fought for freedom.
activity_question: Where were the unidentified soldiers buried?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to honor unknown soldiers?

title: A Giant Tribute to Honest Abe
story: Crowds cheered as the Lincoln Memorial was unveiled in 1922. Chief Justice William Howard Taft led the ceremony on May 30, honoring America's 16th president. The massive marble statue of Abraham Lincoln sits inside a grand temple-like building. It quickly became a symbol of unity and freedom for the nation.
activity_question: Who led the dedication ceremony for the Lincoln Memorial?
bonus_fact: The Lincoln Memorial has 36 columns, one for each state in the Union at the time of Lincoln's death.

title: Spelling Champion's Tricky Triumph
story: Wendy Guey faced a tough challenge in 1996. At the 69th National Spelling Bee on May 30, she encountered a word that would make most people stumble: vivisepulture. But Wendy didn't flinch. She carefully spelled out each letter, securing her victory and becoming the spelling champion. Her win showed that with practice and determination, even the trickiest words can be conquered.
activity_question: What was the winning word Wendy Guey spelled correctly?
follow_up_question: What do you think vivisepulture might mean?
